Article.ArticleTitle: Shining a light on hidden PFAS: Development of a UV-reducible organic fluorine assay for the rapid screening of PFAS in AFFF, water, soils, and commercial products.

Abstract.AbstractText: Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) continue to pollute the environment, creating demand for efficient analytical methods for their characterization and quantification. To address this, we modified a UV light-activated remediation technology (UV-activated sulfite/iodide) to completely mineralize PFAS, enabling the analytical determination of reducible organic fluorine (ROF). This UV-ROF assay was then adapted for integration with the UV-H2O2-TOP assay, creating a tandem UV-H2O2-TOP-ROF assay. This streamlined approach more comprehensively characterizes PFAS and organic fluorine concentrations, employing liquid chromatography tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) for post-oxidation PFAS analysis and a fluoride-ion selective electrode for post-reduction fluoride measurement. Importantly, free fluoride concentrations measured after the ROF assay were found to align closely with PFAS concentrations quantified post-TOP assay, validating the method's accuracy. We applied this tandem assay to diverse samples, including aqueous film-forming foam (AFFF), ion exchange resin regenerant still bottoms, and commercial products like textiles and cosmetics. High-resolution mass spectrometry and an expanded analytical suite enabled the detection of ultra-short-chain perfluoroalkyl carboxylates (PFCAs) and fluorotelomer compounds such as 5:3/5:1:2 fluorotelomer betaine and 6:2 fluorotelomer sulfonamido betaine. Finally, we developed an analytical decision-making framework to guide future applications of this method for complex PFAS-impacted samples. The work presented herein provides researchers with an inexpensive screening level analytical methodology capable of measuring the reducible PFAS concentration in a sample without the need to use LC-MS/MS measurements. This developed method will benefit researchers requiring rapid, on-site screening of materials suspected of containing high concentrations of PFAS, such as AFFFs, cosmetic products, textiles, AFFF, ion exchange resin regenerant still bottoms, or source-zone soils.
